message:
test(readme): DataFrame.at + .iat scalar access coverage

Pins frankenpandas-fd90.57: DataFrame exposes .at(label, column)
for label-based scalar reads and .iat(row_pos, col_pos) for
position-based scalar reads — pandas-parity surface that lets a
caller pull a single cell value without slicing through getitem +
indexing. Series-level .at/.iat were already covered, but the
DataFrame versions had no integration test.

The new readme_dataframe_at_iat_scalar_access case asserts:

* .at on a DataFrame with default RangeIndex labels returns the
  Scalar at (IndexLabel::Int64(0), "price") — Float64(185.50) —
  proving column lookup by name + label-based row resolution.
* .at(IndexLabel::Int64(2), "volume") returns Int64(800), proving
  the second-from-last row + the third column also resolve.
* .iat(0, 1) and .iat(2, 2) return the same values via positional
  access, proving column ordinals match CSV header order
  (ticker=0, price=1, volume=2) and rows are 0-indexed.
* Out-of-bounds row position .iat(99, 0) returns Err.
* Missing label .at(IndexLabel::Int64(99), "price") returns Err.
* Missing column .at(IndexLabel::Int64(0), "ghost_col") returns Err.

Beads: chore JSONL flush capturing fd90.57 in_progress; closes on
the next br sync after assertion lands.
